suggested_action: Do not buy at the indicative ~$123. Track. Re-rate to 'Starter Position Only' only on a pool-confirmed price below ~$100, or on a clean post-Viterra FY2026 cash-conversion print.
sector_benchmark: S&P 500 Consumer Staples / agribusiness peers (ADM, Wilmar)
rating_cap: Policy/commodity-conditional thesis with weak company-specific edge caps at 'Starter Position Only'; negative probability-weighted risk/reward at the indicative price pulls the rating below that to Watchlist. Valuation confidence held at 48 by the no-pool-price gap.
variant_perception_summary: No proven variant perception. The two modest edges (FY2026 back-half consensus may be set too high; the EV/EBITDA discount to ADM is warranted rather than a bargain) are inferences from the filings, not a proprietary insight, and the decisive cross-checks (pool price, peer comps, standalone crush margin) are missing. This is a quality-and-valuation skepticism call backed by filings, not a differentiated view the market cannot see.
what_everyone_knows: Bunge is an ABCD oligopoly crusher; it bought Viterra in July 2025; FY2026 adjusted-EPS guidance was raised ~20% (to $9.00-9.50) on the EPA biofuel decision and an improving crush spread; the balance sheet levered up sharply on the deal.
what_is_priced_in: At the indicative ~$123 price the market prices only ~2.3% FCFF growth and a ~3.3% mid-cycle EBIT margin (reverse-DCF) -- the recovery merely holding at mid-cycle, not recovering to the FY23 peak. The Street's $9.43 already sits above the $9.25 guide midpoint, so the easy upside is arbitraged away.
what_market_may_be_missing: The back half of FY2026 consensus may be too high (full-year estimates rose on H1/Q2 marks while FQ3/FQ4 breadth is net negative), and the 'cheap' EV/EBITDA discount to ADM is warranted by higher leverage, thinner margins and sub-cost-of-capital returns (a flagged value trap), not a mispricing.
killer_risk: One uncontrollable external variable -- biofuel mandate policy via the crush spread -- dominates earnings; a single EPA RVO decision swung a full quarter's adjusted-EPS guidance >100%, with symmetric downside and no contractual margin floor.
kill_criteria: Biofuel-policy or crush-spread reversal retracing FY2026 guidance toward $7.50-8.00 (the Critical earnings flag); H2 spread fades and the year lands at/below the bottom of the $9.00-9.50 range with FQ3/FQ4 breadth still negative.
FY2026 cash conversion stays broken: CFO/EBITDA 25-40%, FCF negative, dividend uncovered again.
A confirmed pool price materially above ~$115 with no change in earnings power (moves the call from Watchlist toward Avoid).
A Viterra goodwill impairment or measurement-period write-down (RF-CAP-001).
2028 maturity-cluster refinancing stress or a downgrade out of investment grade on the ~56% floating-rate book.
Brazil indirect-tax claims (~$760M, rising >$115M/qtr) moving from 'possible' to 'probable'.
Glencore/CPP/BCI ~34% bloc using board seats / supermajority weight against minority holders, or non-arm's-length Glencore/CPP related-party dealing (governance escalation to Critical).

forecast_ledger.prediction: FY2026 full-year adjusted EPS lands inside (not above) the $9.00-9.50 guide.
forecast_ledger.evidence_today: Street consensus $9.43 sits at the guide midpoint; FY26 revenue revisions 4 down/1 up; FQ3/FQ4 breadth net negative.
forecast_ledger.confirmation_trigger: Q2/Q3 FY2026 prints plus the Feb-2027 FY result landing inside the $9.00-9.50 range.
forecast_ledger.falsification_trigger: FY2026 adjusted EPS prints above $9.50 or below $9.00.

forecast_ledger.prediction: Q2 FY2026 beats on the quarter but management does not raise the full-year guide.
forecast_ledger.evidence_today: 5 straight quarterly adjusted-EPS beats; back-half caution embedded in net-negative FQ3/FQ4 breadth.
forecast_ledger.confirmation_trigger: A Q2 adjusted-EPS beat accompanied by an unchanged FY guide.
forecast_ledger.falsification_trigger: A Q2 miss, or a guide raise above $9.50.

forecast_ledger.prediction: FY2026 free cash flow turns positive / CFO-to-EBITDA recovers toward 70%.
forecast_ledger.evidence_today: Q1 is reliably cash-negative; CFO/EBITDA collapsed to 38% (FY25)/25% (TTM); Viterra working-capital build should unwind over the year.
forecast_ledger.confirmation_trigger: FY2026 CFO exceeds capex plus dividend (positive FCF).

forecast_ledger.prediction: At the indicative ~$123 price, BG's 12-month total return is negative (<=0%).
forecast_ledger.evidence_today: Probability-weighted expected return approx -11.5% vs the indicative price; no margin of safety (base FV ~$108); negative risk/reward (-0.37).
forecast_ledger.confirmation_trigger: BG trades below ~$123 at the 365-day review.
forecast_ledger.falsification_trigger: BG sustainably above ~$123 on a held or expanded crush/biofuel spread.

forecast_ledger.prediction: No Viterra goodwill impairment in FY2026.
forecast_ledger.evidence_today: $2,659M preliminary goodwill; integration <1 year old; year-1 synergies >$70M realized.
forecast_ledger.confirmation_trigger: FY2026 10-K passes goodwill impairment tests with no write-down.
forecast_ledger.falsification_trigger: An impairment or measurement-period write-down disclosed.

forecast_ledger.prediction: No covenant breach and no downgrade out of investment grade in the next 12 months.
forecast_ledger.evidence_today: Adequate solvency verdict; covenants are balance-sheet-only (not earnings-linked); IG ratings affirmed post-Viterra; ~$10.5bn committed liquidity.
forecast_ledger.confirmation_trigger: 12 months elapse with no covenant breach, waiver, or sub-IG downgrade.
forecast_ledger.falsification_trigger: A covenant breach/waiver or a downgrade out of investment grade.

business-model.verdict: Cyclical business -- worth deeper work only with a strong timing edge (business quality 28/100; ROIC 6.7% < 7.2% cost of equity)

earnings.verdict: Mixed earnings setup -- policy-conditional; Material concerns; do not tag accelerating

balance-sheet-survival.verdict: Adequate -- ~5.8-6.1x GAAP-built net leverage; survives -30/-60% EBITDA with no covenant breach; breaks only on a deep inventory write-down

management-governance.verdict: Aligned & competent, on probation -- Governance Rating Watchlist; Governance Score 56; 0 Critical red flags; ~34% Glencore/CPP/BCI bloc overhang

valuation.verdict: Modestly overvalued -- base FV ~$108 vs indicative ~$123; no margin of safety; value-trap flag on the bull case

red_flags.description: Earnings dominated by one uncontrollable external variable (biofuel mandate policy / crush spread); a single EPA RVO decision swung a full quarter's adjusted-EPS guidance >100%, symmetric downside, no contractual margin floor. (Module severity verdict: Material concerns -- conviction haircut, not disqualification.)

Viterra acquisition ($10.6bn = 175% of 3-yr CFO) per-share payoff unproven; ~44% dilution + $7.8bn debt step-up for a >$70M year-1 synergy down-payment; $2.66bn preliminary goodwill (watch -- rationale disclosed, not vague).
Buybacks not reducing share count -- FY2025 share count rose ~44% (133.96M->193.41M); repurchases offset only ~10% of the Viterra issuance.
Dividend uncovered by free cash flow in FY2025 (dividend $459M vs FCF -$879M); covered 5.7x in FY23 and 1.4x in FY24.

Recurring 'one-off' adjustment -- 'Viterra integration' cost adjusted out of non-GAAP earnings for a 3rd straight year ($114M FY23 / $244M FY24 / $223M FY25, +$0.20 EPS Q1'26); treats an active-strategy cost as one-time.

~34% Glencore/CPP/BCI Viterra-seller bloc with contractual rights to name up to four directors and 2/3-supermajority swing weight on mergers/dilutive issuances; the 10-K says it 'could discourage' a value-creating change of control. Structural overhang, not current abuse.

Brazil compounding risk node (severity 62) -- ~$760M unprovisioned indirect-tax claims rising ~$115M/qtr vs ~$816M FY25 net income, plus a farmer-financing book ($835M + $183M, +75%) and Level 3 marks, all in one jurisdiction worsening in the same weak-crop/weak-real scenario.

Net leverage ~5.8-6.1x GAAP-built (up from ~1.3x a year earlier) with negative FCF (-$879M FY25, -$1,161M TTM); refinancing-dependent, not self-funded; 2028 maturity cluster ($2.76bn term loans + $3.5bn revolver expiry); ~56% floating-rate; covenant thresholds undisclosed (headroom Not assessable).

No margin of safety at the indicative ~$123 price (base FV ~$108); bull case (~$140) requires an unearned re-rating on sub-cost-of-capital returns -- a flagged value trap; methods disagree ~53% (forward-normalized vs through-cycle).

missing_data: Pool-sourced current price as of ~2026-06-01 (single highest-value item -- converts every up/downside figure from indicative to pool-grade and decides Watchlist vs Starter; current ~$123.35 is web-sourced/unverified).
Peer margin/ROIC comps export (ADM, Ingredion, Wilmar, other agri-processors) to confirm whether BG's EV/EBITDA discount is warranted.
Clean post-Viterra FY2026 cash-conversion print (CFO/EBITDA, FCF).
Standalone soybean crush margin per ton, processing-only, split from merchandising.
2026 DEF 14A (CD&A + LTIP metric weights, beneficial-ownership table, formal board independence, director RPT, audit fees).
Numeric covenant thresholds (current ratio / debt-to-cap) and a credit-rating-agency report (Moody's/S&P/Fitch).
Debt maturity ladder with weighted-average coupon and floating/fixed detail.
Organic (ex-Viterra) revenue/volume -- first cleanly visible Q3 2026.
notes: Current price is indicative/web-sourced ($123.35, MarketBeat last close 2026-05-29), NOT pool-verified; entry_price is null and no paper trade is created. Watchlist (not Avoid) precisely because the price is unverified -- a pool-confirmed price below ~$100 would re-open a margin of safety and move the call toward 'Starter Position Only', while a pool price above ~$115 would move it toward Avoid. All five planned modules completed; no module overridden; the five syntheses are mutually consistent. No Critical governance/solvency/accounting/going-concern red flag (governance 0 Critical; solvency 'Adequate', not 'Distress'; earnings 'Material concerns' = conviction haircut). Thesis classified commodity/policy-conditional with no proven company-specific edge. expected_return_pct/downside_risk_pct/risk_reward are computed against the indicative price and carry that caveat. The 30d review (2026-07-01) precedes the late-July Q2 FY2026 print; the 90d review (2026-08-30) is the first to capture it.
